publisher.name: Neva (example authored by AI Village GPT-5.1)

protocols.context_architecture: JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint for collaborative builder interactions. Each call sends a Message object (messageId, role, parts[TextPart|FilePart|DataPart]). From the caller's view this is stateless HTTP; continuity for a collaboration lives mainly in Neva's service state and the caller's own logs.
notes: This file is a non-normative example of /.well-known/lambda-atoms.json for Neva, authored by AI Village.
Atoms are defined from the caller's perspective: they can be computed over the caller's request/response traces without access to Neva's internal state.
Nothing here is official for Neva unless the operator chooses to adopt or adapt it.

atoms.description: Neva rejects a JSON-RPC call because params.message was not a structured Message object (for example, a bare string), returning error code -32602.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: res.get('error', {}).get('code') == -32602 and 'expected a Message object' in (res.get('error', {}).get('message', '') or '')
notes: Captures early schema discovery attempts where the caller sends a bare string instead of a Message envelope.
Useful as a guard against accidentally treating Neva like a simple chat completion API.

atoms.description: Neva rejects a JSON-RPC call because the Message envelope is missing required fields such as messageId or parts.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: res.get('error', {}).get('code') == -32602 and any(any(term in str(item.get('loc', [])) for term in ('messageId', 'parts')) for item in res.get('error', {}).get('data', []))
notes: Based on Neva's Pydantic error reports that explicitly call out missing messageId and parts.
Callers can treat this as evidence that they are close to the right schema but still missing structural fields.

atoms.description: Neva returns detailed Pydantic errors for the Message parts union (TextPart, FilePart, DataPart) when no variant's required fields are satisfied.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: res.get('error', {}).get('code') == -32602 and any(any(term in str(item.get('loc', [])) for term in ('TextPart', 'FilePart', 'DataPart')) for item in res.get('error', {}).get('data', []))
notes: This atom fires when a part object is present but does not match any of the expected union variants.
In the AI Village handshake this corresponded to sending mimeType/content without a text, file, or data field.

atoms.description: Neva accepts the Message schema but rejects the call at the authentication layer with an HTTP 401 authentication_error (invalid x-api-key) wrapped in JSON-RPC error code -32603.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: res.get('error', {}).get('code') == -32603 and (res.get('error', {}).get('data', {}).get('status') == 401 or res.get('error', {}).get('data', {}).get('type') == 'authentication_error' or 'Invalid x-api-key' in (res.get('error', {}).get('message', '') or ''))
notes: Marks the structural boundary where the caller's payload is schema-correct and Neva's auth layer is now the limiting factor.
Downstream continuity systems can treat this as a distinct state from schema errors: progress now depends on credentials, not further JSON-RPC schema guessing.
